highlights.text: I was looking for this rip to continue into yestrerday, writing on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can do more back-testing.
My general lean is to defer to the trend.

While seasonals may have given this rally an extra tailwind, it had a clear technical cause as well, and its the same sequence I have discussed everyday in this newsletter for years and the same sequence that produces every parabolic rally in ES: What I call the two siblings or “elevator down” into “short squeeze”.
I call this elevator down , and we saw it Thursday where ES sold 250 points in a session.
The trigger for this squeeze is always the same: A Failed Breakdown or Level Reclaim.

On Friday at 10:40AM we tested 6543, recovered 6553 shortly after, and were off to the races (this setup was a re-test of a big Failed Breakdown that occurred at 5:30AM Friday morning - which I discussed extensively on Friday).
Today, I was looking for more - but we’d need that elevator down sell first to trap bears and fuel the move.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll talk the sequence of Failed Breakdowns that produced the squeeze we saw this week.

post_title: The Seasonal Thanksgiving SPX Rally Has Delivered. Can Bulls Keep It Going Until Friday? Nov 26 Plan
